start.speaker: Apollyon
start.text: Where do you think you are going, deserter? You were born in my city, wore my colors, and served me with more eagerness than you now admit. I know the promises you broke and the sins you once defended.

choices.text: All of that is true. And all of it was carried to the Cross behind me.
My knees shake, but I will not make fear my master again.

accuse.speaker: Apollyon
accuse.text: A Cross? You think a hill and borrowed words undo what you are? You will fail again. You always do. Come back before your new Master is ashamed of you.

choices.text: I may stumble, but I am not kept by my strength. You have no claim on a pardoned pilgrim.
You speak my old name. I answer to the One who renamed me.

rage.speaker: Apollyon
rage.text: Then stand there and be broken. If I cannot own you, I will drown you in fear and shame.

choices.text: Stand firm, pray, and answer with promise. (The fight begins.)
